phase1_details.reason: Both involve splitting a combined column containing two pieces of information (hostname+region vs IP+hostname) into separate fields for downstream processing. The specific fields differ but the scenario and conversation would be nearly identical — parsing a combined text field into two columns for filtering/ingestion.

Both involve merging separate first name and last name columns into a single full name field before sharing/exporting a report. The domain differs (grants vs school counseling) but the task, data structure, and conversation would be essentially identical.

Both involve checking whether a specific identifier (grant code vs student ID) exists across multiple datasets before submitting a consolidated/de-duplicated report. The conversation flow and operations would be interchangeable.

Both involve analyzing month-over-month or week-over-week growth trends in a key metric (user growth vs program enrollment) to present to investors/board. The analytical task and conversation structure are essentially the same.

Both involve bulk-correcting inconsistently entered categorical/status values in a contact or donor dataset before importing into a downstream system (CRM/mail merge). The scenario, data issue, and resolution are nearly identical.

Both involve reshaping a wide-format dataset with separate columns per category (survey questions vs test types) into a long/tidy format for charting. The core transformation task and conversation would be essentially the same.

Both scenarios are about checking whether a specific required code exists in a dataset prior to submitting it (program area code in a grant submission vs course code in a master schedule). The user action and validation task are effectively the same: confirm presence of required code(s) before deadline/submission.

phase2_details.reason: Both involve defining/saving a reusable metric and then looking it up to confirm its definition for standardized reporting. dana_001 (cloud spend metric) and dana_096 (utilization metric) are in different domains, but the core scenario — creating then verifying a reusable metric definition — is essentially the same workflow. However, the domains differ enough (cloud IT vs staffing/operations) that conversations would not be interchangeable. Withdrawing this cluster.

dana_001 is building a reusable cloud spend metric; dana_006 is looking up an existing 'cloud waste' metric definition. Same domain (cloud governance/IT) and same goal (understanding a saved metric definition for reporting). These are close but dana_001 is about creating and dana_006 is about looking up — different user actions. Withdrawing.

Both involve a healthcare manager needing a vetted, standardized metric definition saved for reuse in reporting — dana_092 for 30-day readmission rate and dana_089 for no-show rate. Same domain (clinical/healthcare operations), same user goal (get an agreed metric definition before sharing reports), same workflow. The conversations would be structurally interchangeable with only the metric name differing.

Both involve checking whether specific codes/values exist across a dataset before a grant submission deadline. dana_041 checks for a grant code across multiple program datasets; dana_046 confirms required program area codes exist in a submission file. Same domain (nonprofit grants), same user action (existence/validation check before submission), same goal. Conversations would be nearly interchangeable.

Both are growth marketer scenarios involving cleaning/standardizing text values in a content-related dataset — dana_074 replaces outdated product names in a content calendar, dana_078 fixes inconsistent capitalization of tags and categories in blog post metadata. Different specific operations (find-replace vs case normalization) and different datasets, so these are NOT true duplicates. Withdrawing.
